records.ety: [F. part; (L. ad) + part part. See Part.]

defs: Separately, in regard to space or company; in a state of separation as to place; aside.
quotes: Others apart sat on a hill retired.
The Lord hath set apart him that is godly for himself.

defs: In a state of separation, of exclusion, or of distinction, as to purpose, use, or character, or as a matter of thought; separately; independently; as, consider the two propositions apart.

quotes: Let Pleasure go, put Care apart.

defs: In two or more parts; asunder; to piece; as, to take a piece of machinery apart.
